text:
Celine Dionhas been revelling in a wave of enthusiasm for live performances following her triumphant Olympic stint in Paris.

At 56, the star enthralled an enormous audience at the base of the Eiffel Tower last month with a dazzling show for the Olympics' opening gala. Post that spectacular turn, buzz is that she's all geared up to delight her followers with more of her acclaimed vocal magic.

The revelation comes on the heels of Dion's recent public announcement that she was grappling with Stiff Person Syndrome, a diagnosis that prompted her hiatus from the music scene in 2022. Celine graced the occasion with Edith Piaf's iconic "Hymne A L'Amour," and a confidant divulged to In Touch: "She's determined to keep proving everyone wrong by performing again. The success of this has only made her even more hungry to perform again. She's working on plans for her next performance."

Battling Stiff Person Syndrome hasn't dampened Celine's resolve; indeed, sources say her return feels like a true miracle. Yet, it signals the beginning rather than the end of her journey to recovery.

A friend deeply involved in Celines career mentioned: "It doesn't mean she's out of the woods or over this horrible disease, there's still a long road ahead. She will need to keep fighting every day towards her healing, but she's not giving up and intends to keep fighting and setting a positive example for her fans and for anyone battling this disease.", reportsthe Mirror.

READ MORE:BBC fans slam Olympics pundits after Men's 200m final - 'Stop making excuses'



Despite her stunning performance at the opening ceremony of the Paris 2024 Olympics, global superstar Celine Dion remained grounded. Just hours after the event, she was spotted taking a leisurely stroll around Paris, dressed casually in a denim hoodie and long-sleeved shirt.

The singer was visibly moved when a fan presented her with a single rose.

Don't miss:Noah Lyles diagnosed with Covid minutes after leaving 200m track in a wheelchair

Don't miss:Bradly Sinden withdraws from taekwondo fight as Team GB miss out on bronze medal

Don't miss:Team GB star 'says horrible things' after missing out on Olympic medal

Contrary to earlier reports that Dion had received a staggering $2million for her Olympic appearance, it has been revealed that this was not the case. According to UNILAD, Olympic organisers confirmed that none of the artists were paid for their performances.

The Olympic Committee issued a statement saying: "This commitment reflects their desire to participate in a historic event for France and for the world of sport."

In the weeks leading up to her first performance in two years, Dion opened up about her health struggles in an interview with Hoda Kotb.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

She described her condition as feeling like "It's like somebody is strangling you," demonstrating the physical sensations by pressing on her throat.

She added: "It's like you're talking like that, and you cannot go higher or lower. It gets into a spasm."

title: Celine Dion's 'next move' after triumphant Olympics performance amid health battle | Celebrity News | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Alexandra Bullard
description: Celine Dion is reportedly planning to organise a series of performances over the next few years after her triumphant return to the stage at the Paris Olympics.
